mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production reflects rising aggregate output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year correlation of INDPRO change vs 60-day forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below a key threshold signals labor market tightening and rising consumer confidence, which supports equity prices over the next month as spending and earnings improve.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% after being above 6.0% within the prior 12 months
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE < 5.0% with prior 12-month high > 6.0%, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a very low federal funds rate creates an expectation of future monetary tightening that reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS < 1%, the mean 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle characterized by rising short-term interest rates, further increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations by raising the risk-free rate and reducing the present value of future cash flows.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation above 3%
failure_condition: In months where the Fed funds rate rises (one-month change > 0) during a tightening cycle, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and consumer durables demand, which foreshadows economic contraction and produces negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: After HOUST MoM declines exceed -10%, the 5-day forward SPX return is not negative with > 60% frequency over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales signals synchronized expansion in labor income and consumer spending, reinforcing corporate revenue and profitability expectations positively.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change > 0 and RSAFS MoM change > 0
failure_condition: In months with both PAYEMS and RSAFS positive MoM, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 40% of cases over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-pandemic recovery regime, elevated retail sales levels relative to trend indicate strong consumer demand that supports corporate earnings and drives equity prices higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: During the post-pandemic recovery period, the 60-day forward SPX return when RSAFS is above its 12-month moving average is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained level of industrial production above its recent norm signals high capacity utilization and economic momentum, which typically associates with positive short-term equity returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: When INDPRO > 105, the 5-day forward SPX return is not positive with statistical significance (one-sample t-test p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.
